# Ai Method Matrix

Converted from `22_ai_method_matrix.csv` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

| method | family | when_to_use | when_not_to_use | why_it_works | mindset | metrics | failure_modes | first_safety_rule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Review mining with LLMs | Research | You have many reviews, comments, forum posts, support tickets, or competitor reviews. | Do not use it as final truth. Use it to find patterns to verify with humans. | LLMs cluster language, objections, jobs, and emotional triggers faster than manual scanning. | Be a forensic analyst. Extract buyer language; do not invent it. | Quote frequency, themes found, verified interviews, conversion lift from using real language. | Hallucinated quotes, overfitting to loud users, missing silent churn reasons. | Ask the model to quote exact source lines and separate evidence from inference. |
| Interview guide generation | Research | You need to run JTBD or buyer interviews and want sharper questions quickly. | Do not let AI interview people without supervision for sensitive/high-value research. | AI can create question ladders, probes, and bias checks from your hypotheses. | Be a disciplined investigator, not a pitchman. | Interview completion, quality of surprising insights, number of falsified assumptions. | Leading questions that confirm your desired answer. | Ask AI to rewrite questions to avoid selling, leading, or assuming. |
| Transcript coding and theme extraction | Research | You have call/interview transcripts and need patterns across objections, triggers, and decision criteria. | Do not replace human review when stakes are high or sample is small. | AI accelerates qualitative coding and can compare segments. | Be a judge weighing evidence. | Intercoder agreement, theme stability, exact quotes, actionability. | Overgeneralizing from a few charismatic quotes. | Force line citations and keep a human-coded sample. |
| Synthetic customer simulation | Research | You need hypotheses before spending money on interviews or ads. | Do not treat synthetic customers as evidence of real demand. | LLMs can simulate plausible perspectives and expose blind spots, but they reflect training data and prompt framing. | Be a war-gamer. Use it to prepare, not decide. | Number of hypotheses generated, later validation rate, discarded bad assumptions. | Believing fake certainty, persona stereotypes. | Label all synthetic output as speculation until validated. |
| Competitor review triangulation | Research | You need to find gaps in competitors’ products, pricing, onboarding, docs, and support. | Do not scrape or use private/prohibited data. | Combines review mining, feature comparison, and objection mapping. | Be an intelligence analyst. | Repeated competitor complaints, angle quality, proof gaps identified. | Cherry-picking weakness while ignoring competitor strengths. | Use multiple sources: G2/Capterra/forums/docs/pricing pages. |
| Support-ticket opportunity mining | Research | You have tickets/chats and need upgrade triggers, churn risk, onboarding friction, and feature demand. | Do not use private customer data in public AI tools without approval. | Tickets reveal real friction in buyers' own language. | Be a product-growth diagnostician. | Ticket categories, support burden reduced, upgrade opportunities, churn themes. | Privacy breach, sampling only angry users. | De-identify data and cluster by job/problem, not only feature request. |
| Intent query clustering | Research | You have search-console, keyword, paid search, or site-search data. | Do not chase keywords unrelated to your ICP or ability to serve. | AI can cluster by intent stage and map content/ad offers. | Be a traffic controller. | High-intent clusters, CAC by cluster, demo/trial rate by intent. | Keyword volume addiction and low-quality leads. | Map every cluster to buyer state and proof asset. |
| Market map synthesis | Research | You need to understand category structure, alternatives, substitutes, and buyer comparison logic. | Do not use unverified AI market maps in investor/customer materials. | AI can synthesize category players, alternatives, and language fast. | Be a cartographer. | Completeness, missing alternatives found, stakeholder validation. | False competitors, stale data. | Ground with public sources and buyer interviews. |
| ICP pressure test | Strategy | You have an ICP hypothesis and need to sharpen who should and should not buy. | Do not broaden ICP because AI found many possible segments. | AI can stress-test urgency, budget, trigger events, and proof needs. | Be ruthless. Narrow until the sales motion becomes obvious. | Segment win rate, sales cycle, activation, retention, support load. | Attractive but unprofitable segments. | Force disqualification criteria. |
| JTBD map generation | Strategy | You need to connect product features to progress customers want to make. | Do not stop at generic pains like 'save time'. | AI can draft job maps and forces/struggles from research inputs. | Be a systems mapper. | Clarity of job, interview confirmation, message relevance. | Invented jobs detached from real buying moments. | Use only real quotes as inputs when possible. |
| Positioning hypothesis lab | Strategy | You need several positioning options before choosing a market frame. | Do not let AI choose positioning by prettiness. | AI can generate category, enemy, alternative, and differentiated-value frames. | Be a strategist choosing a battlefield. | Message comprehension, recall, qualified conversion, sales feedback. | Me-too claims, impossible-to-prove differentiation. | Tie every claim to evidence and buyer alternative. |
| Proof architecture design | Strategy | You know claims but not what proof must surround them. | Do not rely on testimonials when buyers need technical/security proof. | AI can map claims to evidence types: demo, docs, reviews, ROI, security, case study. | Be an evidence architect. | Proof completeness, drop-off reduction, sales objections reduced. | Claim-proof mismatch. | For every claim ask: what would a skeptical buyer inspect next? |
| Objection-to-asset matrix | Strategy | Sales hears recurring objections. | Do not answer every objection with more copy; sometimes fix product/pricing. | AI converts objections into needed assets and tests. | Be a friction eliminator. | Objection frequency, win rate, sales-cycle reduction. | Defending weaknesses instead of resolving them. | Classify objections: misunderstanding, risk, missing capability, pricing, trust. |
| Category narrative builder | Strategy | You need to explain why the old way is breaking and why the category matters now. | Do not manufacture false urgency or fear. | AI can generate narrative arcs and evidence needs. | Be a teacher of change. | Audience comprehension, content engagement, inbound quality. | Dramatic but unsupported narratives. | Back the narrative with sources and customer reality. |
| Pricing-page critique | Strategy | You need to reduce pricing confusion and increase confidence. | Do not hide fees or manipulate plans. | AI can inspect clarity, plan boundaries, risk reversal, and upgrade logic. | Be a risk reducer. | Pricing-page conversion, sales questions, refund/churn, plan-fit accuracy. | Dark patterns, bait-and-switch, hidden limits. | Require plain-language total cost and who each plan is for. |
| AI feature value framing | Strategy | You sell AI features and need to avoid AI-washing. | Do not lead with 'AI-powered' if the buyer cares about outcome and risk. | AI can translate technical capability into buyer jobs, limitations, and proof. | Be concrete: what task changes, how much, and what risk remains? | AI feature adoption, time-to-value, retention, support tickets. | Overclaiming autonomous capability. | State limitation and human-control boundaries. |
| Angle matrix generation | Creative | You need many honest ways to frame the same offer for testing. | Do not create manipulative fear/shame angles. | AI can combine jobs, emotions, proof types, and channels into testable angles. | Be a portfolio manager of hypotheses. | CTR, CVR, lead quality, downstream activation. | Volume without insight; repetitive AI slop. | Generate angles from real research; kill weak ones fast. |
| Hook stress test | Creative | You have ad hooks and need to identify vague, hypey, or unbelievable claims. | Do not optimize hooks only for clicks. | AI can evaluate specificity, inspectability, skepticism, and buyer fit. | Be skeptical like your buyer. | Hook believability, qualified CTR, bounce rate, comments. | Clickbait that poisons trust. | Ask: what proof does this hook force us to show? |
| Ad variant factory | Creative | You need structured ad variants across pain, proof, demo, comparison, and risk-reversal angles. | Do not flood channels without a test design. | AI lowers production cost; testing determines truth. | Be an experimenter, not a spammer. | Incremental conversions, CAC, creative fatigue, lead quality. | Confounded tests and brand dilution. | Limit variables: one hypothesis per variant group. |
| Landing page proof rewrite | Creative | You have traffic but skeptical visitors are not converting. | Do not solve a broken product or wrong audience with copy. | AI can reorganize pages by buyer questions, proof, objections, and CTA sequence. | Be a guide through uncertainty. | Scroll depth, demo/trial clicks, conversion rate, sales-fit. | Overlong pages with no real proof. | Map each section to a buyer question. |
| Demo script generation | Creative | You need product demos that show the buyer's job, not feature inventory. | Do not create fake demos that hide constraints. | AI can structure demos around before/after workflow and decision criteria. | Be a show-don't-tell operator. | Demo completion, sales conversion, activation from demo viewers. | Demo theatre; no operational reality. | Use real workflows and name limitations. |
| Video storyboard / UGC concepting | Creative | You need creative concepts for short-form or explainer videos. | Do not fake customers or endorsements. | AI can draft scenes, cuts, hooks, and proof beats. | Be a director of evidence, not spectacle. | View-through, qualified clicks, watch time, trial starts. | Synthetic authenticity that feels fake. | Anchor every scene in a real job or proof point. |
| Founder-led content drafting | Creative | You need expertise-rich content that earns trust. | Do not let AI manufacture personal experience. | AI can outline, challenge, and polish; founder must supply judgment and stories. | Be the expert; AI is the editor. | Engagement by ICP, replies, referrals, branded search. | Generic thought leadership. | Require specific examples, tradeoffs, failures, and lessons. |
| GEO / AI-answer content brief | Creative | You need content that answer engines can cite and summarize accurately. | Do not stuff keywords or write for bots only. | AI can structure clear definitions, comparisons, evidence, FAQs, and citations. | Be a librarian building reliable evidence objects. | AI search citations, referral quality, branded queries, citation share. | Thin AI spam, hallucinated claims. | Use primary sources, schema, clear authorship, and dated facts. |
| Case study builder | Creative | You need proof assets from customer wins. | Do not invent metrics or cherry-pick nonrepresentative success. | AI can structure problem, context, intervention, result, tradeoffs, and implementation details. | Be a witness preparing evidence. | Case study influenced pipeline, sales-cycle reduction, objection reduction. | Vague success stories with no baseline. | Include baseline, constraints, numbers, and buyer quotes. |
| Comparison page generator | Creative | Buyers compare you with alternatives. | Do not attack competitors unfairly or hide where they are better. | AI can organize fair comparison tables, use cases, migration considerations, and fit/no-fit guidance. | Be a trusted advisor. | Comparison-page conversion, sales assist, AI-search visibility. | Biased comparison that destroys trust. | Include 'choose competitor when...' sections. |
| Documentation-to-marketing repurposing | Creative | Technical docs contain buyer proof. | Do not turn precise docs into vague slogans. | AI can turn docs into buyer explainers, diagrams, FAQs, and demo scripts. | Be a translator, not a simplifier of truth. | Docs-assisted conversion, developer activation, reduced support questions. | Loss of technical accuracy. | Have SMEs review every technical claim. |
| Segment-specific page generation | Personalization | You have distinct ICPs with different jobs and proof needs. | Do not create thousands of shallow pages. | AI helps adapt messaging and proof to segment context. | Be relevant without becoming creepy. | Segment CVR, time on page, pipeline quality. | Thin duplicate content, privacy concerns. | Use segment-level context, not invasive personal data. |
| Account-based message drafting | Personalization | You have target accounts and public context. | Do not imply knowledge from private surveillance. | AI can tailor outreach around public triggers, tech stack, initiatives, and likely jobs. | Be useful, specific, and respectful. | Reply rate, meeting quality, unsubscribe/spam rate. | Creepy personalization, hallucinated company facts. | Only use verifiable public facts and cite internal sources. |
| Lifecycle email sequence design | Lifecycle | Users sign up but fail to activate or upgrade. | Do not bombard users who have not shown intent. | AI can map emails to activation events, objections, and milestones. | Be a coach guiding progress. | Activation, feature adoption, trial-to-paid, unsubscribes. | Too much email, low relevance. | Trigger from behavior and value milestones. |
| In-app nudge copy | Lifecycle | Users are in product and need help taking next useful action. | Do not interrupt deep work or use guilt/pressure. | AI can produce concise nudges by state and goal. | Be a helpful guide inside the task. | Feature adoption, completion rate, nudge dismissal rate. | Manipulative interruptions. | Use reversible, dismissible, context-aware prompts. |
| Churn-risk message generation | Lifecycle | A user shows inactivity or downgrade risk. | Do not use fear or make cancellation hard. | AI can tailor help, education, and save offers to likely reason. | Be a problem solver, not a hostage taker. | Retention lift, save rate, satisfaction after save. | Annoying users who want to leave. | Make cancellation clear; offer help, data export, or downgrade. |
| Customer education content lab | Lifecycle | Buyers need to become successful users after purchase. | Do not treat education as upsell spam. | AI can create tutorials, checklists, examples, and role-specific enablement. | Be the adoption architect. | Time-to-value, completion, support tickets, expansion. | Generic content no one uses. | Tie content to activation milestones and jobs. |
| Sales-call brief generator | Sales | A rep needs to understand account context and likely objections quickly. | Do not use unverified AI guesses as facts. | AI summarizes public data, CRM notes, prior calls, and likely agenda. | Be prepared, not performative. | Call quality, discovery depth, next-step conversion. | Hallucinated facts and overpersonalization. | Separate verified facts from hypotheses. |
| Objection handler / battlecard | Sales | Reps need concise answers to competitor, pricing, security, and implementation concerns. | Do not script manipulative pressure lines. | AI can generate answer paths and proof assets. | Be a calm expert lowering risk. | Win rate in common objection scenarios, rep adoption. | Robotic scripts and unsupported claims. | Link every answer to proof. |
| Proposal / RFP assistant | Sales | You need to respond consistently and fast to structured buyer requirements. | Do not auto-submit unreviewed answers, especially legal/security claims. | AI drafts from approved knowledge base and past answers. | Be exact and compliant. | Response time, error rate, win rate, legal rework. | Fabricated commitments. | Use retrieval from approved source only and SME review. |
| Post-demo follow-up personalization | Sales | A buyer attended a demo and needs a useful next step. | Do not send generic recap spam. | AI can map demo topics to proof links, next actions, and stakeholder-specific notes. | Be a project manager for buyer confidence. | Reply rate, stakeholder forwards, next meeting conversion. | Overlong recaps and false promises. | Include only what was discussed plus relevant proof. |
| Customer success expansion map | Sales/CS | You want ethical expansion based on proven usage and unmet jobs. | Do not upsell before value is achieved. | AI can identify account patterns, missing workflows, and expansion hypotheses. | Be a steward of customer value. | Expansion rate, NRR, satisfaction, usage depth. | Pushing features instead of outcomes. | Tie expansion to customer goals and demonstrated value. |
| Experiment design assistant | Measurement | You need to test AI-generated creatives, pages, emails, or workflows. | Do not let AI pick winners from underpowered data. | AI can draft hypotheses, sample needs, guardrails, and interpretation checks. | Be a scientist of buyer behavior. | Test validity, lift, confidence/credible intervals, downstream quality. | Peeking, multiple-comparison false positives. | Pre-register hypothesis and success metrics. |
| Attribution critique agent | Measurement | Your dashboard says a campaign works but you are unsure if it is incremental. | Do not equate last-click ROAS with causal lift. | AI can interrogate measurement assumptions and suggest holdouts/lift tests. | Be suspicious of convenient numbers. | Incrementality evidence, channel mix quality, CAC payback. | Dashboard worship. | Ask: what would have happened without this? |
| Creative fatigue monitor | Measurement | High-performing ads decline over time. | Do not react to noise with constant random changes. | AI can detect pattern shifts and suggest new variants by winning theme. | Be a portfolio manager. | Frequency, CTR decay, CPA drift, qualitative comments. | Over-rotating before statistical signal. | Refresh within proven angles before abandoning them. |
| Insight extraction from experiments | Measurement | You have results but need learning, not just winner/loser. | Do not overread tiny differences. | AI can summarize patterns by segment, channel, and creative angle. | Be a learning accountant. | Reusable insights, future test quality, documented decisions. | Post-hoc storytelling. | Separate confirmed, suggestive, and speculative insights. |
| AI workflow ROI model | Measurement | You are adopting AI internally for marketing/sales productivity. | Do not measure only tool subscription cost or time saved. | AI can model time, quality, error, governance, and revenue effects. | Be an operator. | Cycle time, quality score, error rate, cost saved, revenue lift. | Ignoring review time and risk costs. | Compare end-to-end workflow, not isolated prompt task. |
| Buyer concierge chatbot | Agentic | Your site has complex buyer questions and docs/pricing/security content. | Do not deploy it when answers can be wrong or compliance-sensitive without guardrails. | A chatbot can reduce search friction and route buyers to proof. | Be an informed guide with visible limits. | Qualified conversations, escalation rate, answer accuracy, demo/trial assists. | Hallucination, privacy leaks, bad handoffs. | Use retrieval from approved docs, human handoff, logs, and refusal rules. |
| Website conversion agent | Agentic | You want AI to recommend next steps based on visitor context. | Do not create manipulative adaptive dark patterns. | Agents can match content/proof to intent and reduce navigation effort. | Be a librarian plus concierge. | Content engagement, conversion, user satisfaction, no. of assisted paths. | Creepy personalization and uncontrolled decisions. | Keep recommendations explainable and reversible. |
| Campaign QA agent | Agentic | You launch many AI-generated campaigns/assets and need safety checks. | Do not rely on it as sole legal/brand review. | AI can check claims, broken links, UTMs, tone, policy, and brand consistency. | Be a compliance inspector. | Error caught before launch, approval speed, rework reduction. | False negatives on legal issues. | Use as first-pass QA, not final authority. |
| Knowledge base/RAG for marketing | Agentic/Ops | Teams repeatedly ask for approved claims, proof, personas, and docs. | Do not include unapproved or stale data. | RAG reduces hallucination by grounding AI in trusted sources. | Be a source librarian. | Answer accuracy, source citation rate, team adoption. | Garbage in/garbage out, stale proof. | Version approved sources and expire old claims. |
| Lead-routing and qualification assistant | Agentic | Inbound volume is high and qualification logic is clear. | Do not automate rejection for high-stakes or ambiguous prospects without review. | AI can summarize fit, urgency, and next best action from forms/chats. | Be a triage operator. | Speed-to-lead, meeting quality, qualification accuracy. | Bias, missed strategic accounts. | Use rules + human review for edge cases. |
| AI shopping / agent feed preparation | Agentic Commerce | Your products may be discovered by AI assistants and shopping/research agents. | Do not optimize only human landing pages. | Machine agents need structured, current product/pricing/policy/proof data. | Be machine-readable and trustworthy. | AI referrals, citation quality, product-data accuracy, checkout completion. | Stale feeds, hallucinated descriptions, missing constraints. | Publish clear feeds, schema, policies, comparisons, and support docs. |
| Claims substantiation checker | Governance | You generate many ads/pages and need to prevent unsupported claims. | Do not publish 'best', 'guaranteed', or quantified claims without proof. | AI can flag risky claims and ask for evidence. | Be a prosecutor against your own hype. | Claim pass rate, legal review findings, correction count. | Overclaiming AI outcomes, unverifiable superiority. | Keep a claim ledger with source/evidence/date/owner. |
| AI disclosure decision tree | Governance | You use AI in customer-facing content or product workflows. | Do not assume disclosure always solves trust or legal issues. | AI can help classify materiality, user expectation, and regulatory risk. | Be transparent where it matters; do not make empty disclosure theatre. | Trust, complaints, compliance findings, conversion effect. | Disclosure that erodes trust without explaining value/control. | Disclose when AI involvement affects user expectations, choices, or rights. |
| Bias and vulnerability review | Governance | Personalization, targeting, or AI advice could exploit or exclude vulnerable users. | Do not use sensitive traits or anxiety/fear-based exploitation. | AI can surface potential harms, but humans own decisions. | Be a guardian of autonomy. | Complaints, exclusion analysis, opt-outs, fairness checks. | Predatory personalization and discrimination. | Review high-risk audiences, claims, and targeting criteria. |
| Prompt-injection/security review | Governance | You deploy public chatbots, RAG agents, or tool-using agents. | Do not give agents tools they do not need. | AI systems can be manipulated through user inputs, retrieved content, or tools. | Be a security architect. | Attack test pass rate, leakage rate, tool misuse incidents. | Excessive agency, data leaks, insecure outputs. | Apply OWASP LLM controls: least privilege, input isolation, output validation. |
| Copyright/IP/source review | Governance | You generate images, video, copy, or training data-derived outputs. | Do not use style imitation, likeness, or copyrighted assets recklessly. | AI can identify potential IP and publicity-rights concerns as first pass. | Be rights-conscious. | Review flags, takedowns, asset approval time. | Accidental copying, unauthorized likeness, brand damage. | Use licensed tools/assets and document provenance. |
